summary:
Fluoride substitution for hydroxyl ions in hydroxyapatite increases crystal stability and reduces solubility, thereby providing greater strength; this contrasts unfavorably with phosphorus-based substitutes due to decreased crystallinity and increased dissolution rates. So,
\boxed{A}. This concise statement captures the essential clinical point regarding the mechanism behind fluoride’s effectiveness while maintaining brevity.
